stack_candidates.name: react-vite-fastapi
stack_candidates.reason: Default for clean-room web plus local Python API prototyping.

stack_candidates.name: react-vite-fastify
stack_candidates.reason: Use when a single TypeScript runtime is preferred.

stack_decision_reason: Selected React/Vite TypeScript web app, FastAPI Python API, Chrome MV3 extension, shared TypeScript contracts/core/collectors, and pnpm workspace for a clean-room web-first rebuild of the uploaded Electron/Next reference package. Electron is reference-only and not selected for the target runtime unless a later explicit stack decision changes it.

commands.install: pnpm install && cd apps/api && python -m pip install -e .[dev]

modules.name: web
local_boundaries: No direct crawling/session logic in UI. Use API/extension contracts.
quality_gates: Route shell covers dashboard, product/keyword/ad/manage/settings categories.

modules.name: api
local_boundaries: No hidden cookie/token storage. Local env/secret store only.
quality_gates: Health route and typed error envelope exist.

modules.name: extension
local_boundaries: Content script cannot access secrets or bypass site controls.

modules.name: contracts
local_boundaries: Contracts are source of truth for API, extension, collectors, and core.

modules.name: core
local_boundaries: Pure transformations separated from IO/session/browser work.
quality_gates: Deterministic fixtures for sourcing, keyword, ranking, ad report, and product-name pipelines.

modules.name: collectors
local_boundaries: Collectors must respect access controls, robots/ToS constraints, rate limits, and user-owned sessions.
Raw collection and normalized output remain separate.
quality_gates: Input/output/error schema exists for Coupang, Naver, 1688/Taobao/AliExpress-class references before implementation.
Selector fallback and blocked-session evidence are documented.

modules.name: reference-analysis
local_boundaries: No source text or branded asset copy into implementation.

workstreams.name: Project Development Bootstrap
workstreams.objective: Create and validate AUTOMETA repo bootstrap, workspace, env template, planning docs, and shared build scripts without overriding module routing.
workflow: Create root package/workspace/env/check files.
Do not decide feature implementation details for app/package modules.
Validate scaffold paths against module/workstream routing.

workstreams.name: Project Frontend Design
workstreams.objective: Own commerce workspace UI route shell, table/form/editor states, responsive layout, and Korean UX copy.
workflow: Review screen goal, route, states, and primary action.
Implement reusable React/Vite components without dropping UX states.
Run build/typecheck or document blockers.
quality_gates: Web build or blocker evidence exists.

workstreams.name: Project Backend API
workstreams.objective: Own FastAPI service boundary, stable envelopes, job/progress/cancel APIs, persistence boundaries, and backend test matrix.
workflow: Define request/response/error contracts.
Keep handler/service/repository responsibilities separated.
Run backend tests or document blockers.
quality_gates: API contract changes have tests or explicit evidence.

workstreams.name: Project Contracts
workstreams.objective: Own shared DTO/schema/event contracts and compatibility notes.
workflow: Define stable public shapes.
Check downstream impact before changing fields.
Document migrations for breaking changes.

workstreams.name: Project Core Pipeline
workstreams.objective: Own deterministic core processing, pipeline state, and idempotent execution contracts.
workflow: Separate pure core logic from IO.
Define retry/cancel/error behavior.
Add deterministic tests for core transformations.

workstreams.name: Project Extension Bridge
workstreams.objective: Own extension permissions, message contracts, content/background boundaries, and privacy review.
workflow: Minimize manifest permissions.
Define message payload/reply/error shapes.
Keep content scripts separated from privileged background logic.

workstreams.name: Project Market Collectors
workstreams.objective: Own commerce market collectors, browser/session boundaries, progress/cancel semantics, and normalized output contracts.
workflow: Define allowed inputs/outputs/errors.
Avoid brittle selector-only assumptions.
Respect session/access-control boundaries.

workstreams.name: Project Reference Mapper
workstreams.objective: Map uploaded Bulsaja/Electron/Next reference package into clean-room route, role, IPC/message, collector, and contract evidence before implementation.
workflow: Inspect uploaded package inventories and recovered source-map file names as read-only evidence.
Extract roles/contracts/fixtures/failure modes without copying source text or assets.
Classify implement-now vs defer-with-contract items before contracts/schema work.

domain_terms.notes: Uploaded app.zip and recovered sourcemap files are read-only references. Extract roles, contracts, route states, and fixtures only.
A bounded run that receives market/query/options, emits progress/cancel events, and returns normalized product candidates.
Site-specific collector with input/output/error contracts and session boundary review.
Keyword/detail/ad/recommendation analysis for product discovery, naming, and listing decisions.
Web UI hub for sourcing, ranking, keyword, ad report, product name, purchase/order, settings, and evidence states.
Credentials, cookies, tokens, browser sessions, and account state stay local/user-controlled and are never embedded in repo docs.

boundaries: AUTOMETA is the target repository root for generated Codex commands unless the user changes it.
Uploaded app.zip, background.js, preload.js, source maps, recovered sources, static chunks, screenshots, logos, and templates are reference-only and must not be copied into product code.

Extract behavior roles, route inventory, IPC/message/API contracts, DTO fields, failure modes, and fixture shape from references; do not copy implementation source text, generated chunk code, branded assets, or copywriting.
Electron-specific IPC is not the target runtime default. Re-express necessary behavior as web/API/extension contracts with explicit privacy and session boundaries.
No mock-success fallback. Failures must surface typed errors, retry/cancel/progress state, and evidence logs.
No hidden credential/session handling. Real IDs, passwords, API keys, cookies, tokens, service-account files, and private keys are local-only inputs.
Do not delete, hide, or exclude requested feature categories without a written defer-with-contract decision and replacement validation path.
Use reference-analysis before contracts/schema, backend API, frontend shell, extension/collectors, and core pipeline work.
quality_gates: validate-generated, validate-scaffold, validate-planning, and validate-dev-flow pass after generation.
cleanroom-audit passes and no app.zip/raw source/chunks/maps/assets are copied into target product code.
WBS order keeps reference-analysis before contracts/schema.
Skill budget remains under max_initial_metadata_chars and hard policy skills remain selected, not optional.
Every market collector has input/output/error contracts and anti-bot/session boundary notes before implementation.
Every UI feature has loading/empty/error/success states and Korean user-facing copy review.
Every backend endpoint returns a stable envelope and has contract tests or explicit blocker evidence.
No no-op tests, unpinned Python dependencies, packageManager @latest, or secret-like values in generated files.
